hero.subtitle: telepace runs voice interviews with your users on autopilot — and hands the structured insights back to your agent, so it can act on them.
hero.footnote: Free tier includes 3 studies / month. No credit card.

heroDemo.q1: Quick one — what does your team do today when you need real user feedback?
heroDemo.q2: Got it. And what usually stops you from doing more of it — time, budget, or something else?
heroDemo.wrap: That's exactly what telepace digs into — in a real voice conversation, at scale. Want the full 60-second experience?

howItWorks.title: From idea to insight in a single conversation.

design.title: Design in chat
design.description: Describe what you want to learn. Our Designer agent proposes an outline in seconds — you refine it in conversation.

send.title: Send by voice, phone, or link
send.description: Push to email, drop a share URL, or place outbound calls. Same interview, five channels.

moderate.title: AI moderates the interview
moderate.description: Every session gets the same careful researcher: warm probing, coverage tracking, escalation when it matters.

insights.title: Insights, ready to act
insights.description: Themes, verbatims, personas — piped back into your agent, Notion, or Linear. No dashboard-scrolling required.

insights.title: Insights that read like a researcher wrote them.
insights.description: Not transcripts to wade through — ranked themes with confidence scores, each backed by verbatim quotes you can trace to the source. This is what a finished study looks like.
insights.cta: See it live in the demo →

t1.title: Price anchors to missing enterprise features

t3.title: Usage-based pricing would beat both tiers

quote.text: Seventy-nine a month is fine — until I realized SSO wasn't included. That's when it started feeling punitive.
quote.attribution: Interview #17 · Head of Product, B2B SaaS · 14:32

panel.exportHint: Piped automatically to wherever your team works

channels.title: Meet respondents where they already are.

agentSurfaces.title: Your agent gets a user researcher.
agentSurfaces.description: telepace ships as an MCP server, a Skill, and a REST API. Wherever your agents live — Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, a custom orchestrator — they can now design studies, dispatch interviews, and read back structured insights.

useCases.title: Built for the people who ask why.

pm.description: Get customer voice into every roadmap review.

researchers.description: Scale 1:1 depth without hiring an army.

growth.description: Interview churned users before they forget why they left.

founders.description: Run 30 interviews the week you're building the MVP.

finalCta.subtitle: Every product decision deserves a hundred users behind it.

hero.lede: Start free. Upgrade when a decision depends on it. Cancel with a click. Every tier includes the full Interviewer agent — we don't paywall quality.

completionDefinition.question: How is a completion defined?
completionDefinition.answer: One respondent finishing the interview end-to-end. Abandons and duplicates don't count.

byoLlmKey.question: Can I bring my own LLM key?
byoLlmKey.answer: Yes. Pro and above support BYO Anthropic, OpenAI, and Azure keys — no markup.

phoneMinutes.question: Do you charge for phone minutes?
phoneMinutes.answer: Voice minutes on the Pro plan pass through at cost (~$0.09/min). Team gets bundled minutes.

cancellation.question: How do I cancel?
cancellation.answer: One click. We prorate to the day and email you a CSV export of every insight.

dataTraining.question: Is my raw data used to train models?
dataTraining.answer: Never. Transcripts stay in your workspace. See our Security page for the full attestation.

cta.title: Try before you decide.
cta.lede: The free tier is enough to run your first real study. No card required.

hero.title: Everything you need — and nothing you don't.
hero.lede: The docs mirror the codebase's discipline: contracts first, then examples, then troubleshooting. Every command, event, and MCP tool is here.

60seconds.body: Create a study, share the URL, watch the first completion arrive.

15minutes.body: Wire the MCP server into Claude Code and let your agent do research.

anHour.body: Full production deploy — event store on Postgres, Redis, Fly.io.

campaigns.body: A study lifecycle: draft → published → closed. Immutable event history.

interviews.body: One respondent completing the outline. Text, voice, phone, or email.

insights.body: Themes clustered from transcripts with representative verbatims.

harness.body: Policy-gated dispatcher between commands and agents.

mcp.body: 5 stateful tools. Compatible with any MCP host.

notion.body: One-way push of insights into a database.

linear.body: Create issues per insight, grouped by theme.

webhooks.body: Any of our 20+ event types, HMAC-signed.

mcpToolSchemas.body: Input/Output Pydantic pairs for every tool.

support.title: We answer support in hours, not days.

hero.title: Your agent gets a <italic>user researcher.</italic>
hero.lede: telepace is a first-class MCP server. Five stateful tools your agent can call the same way a human PM would open a research tab.

tools.title: Every tool returns a <italic>next_actions</italic> field. That is what makes tool-using agents effective.

items.createCampaign: Draft a study from a goal + background. Returns a persistent campaign_id.
items.getCampaignProgress: Read the projection: completions so far, coverage per question, spend to date.
items.getCampaignInsights: Return the current cluster of themes with representative verbatims.
items.askFollowup: Mine the transcript corpus with a natural-language question. Non-destructive.
items.pushInsights: Export to Notion, Linear, Slack, or a webhook. Idempotent per destination.

tryIt.title: Wire telepace into Claude Code.
tryIt.body: One command, no config. Your Claude gains an entire research team.

hero.lede: telepace isn't 'a chatbot doing surveys.' It's a harness of specialized agents, an event-sourced spine, and policy layers that keep them honest — designed to be inspected, replayed, and improved.

team.title: Each agent has one job. That's why the system stays reliable.

designer.name: Designer
designer.role: Chats with you to turn a goal into an interview outline.

interviewer.name: Interviewer
interviewer.role: Runs the live conversation. Probes. Tracks coverage. Knows when to stop.

analyst.name: Analyst
analyst.role: Embeds transcripts, clusters themes, drafts insight reports.

coordinator.name: Coordinator
coordinator.role: Watches the campaign as a whole. Nudges, escalates, closes.

harness.title: Not just LLMs in a trenchcoat.
harness.paragraph1Rest: : a policy-gated dispatcher backed by an append-only event store. Nothing gets to an agent until the budget check, the PII check, and the escalation check say yes.
harness.paragraph2: Because state is events, we can replay any campaign, A/B a policy against a corpus, or reconstruct exactly what the Interviewer heard on turn 27 of interview 84.
harness.paragraph3: It's the engineering discipline your compliance team asks about — built in from day one.

budget.v: Per-campaign USD ceilings on LLM + voice minutes.

pii.v: Presidio + custom patterns. Redact before persistence.

escalation.v: Distress / self-harm signals stop the run and page a human.

observability.v: Every agent call has trace_id, cost, and eval score attached.

cta.title: Bring your own agent host.
cta.body: telepace ships as an MCP server + REST + Skill. Wherever your agents live, they get a research team.

hero.lede: Voice isn't a bolt-on. It's how the best user researchers work — because words on a screen leave the deepest signal on the floor. telepace ships production-grade voice on day one.

whyVoice.title: People say more when they don't have to type it.
whyVoice.paragraph1: Traditional research knows this: a 30-minute call yields 3–5× the actionable signal of a written survey. Voice removes the friction of composition, which is where nuance dies.
whyVoice.paragraph2: telepace runs the same warmth end-to-end — greeting, probe, silence, follow-up, close — with the consistency only software can offer. Nobody's tired at 4 PM. Nobody's off-script.
whyVoice.paragraph3: Because we're stream-based (not turn-based), interviews feel like a real conversation — not the polite waiting game of most voice bots.

language.note: EN · ES · FR · DE · JA · ZH · PT · IT · KO · RU

channelsSection.title: Meet respondents where they already are.

browser.name: Browser voice
browser.body: WebRTC + streaming STT. Respondent clicks a link, presses hold-to-talk, done.

phone.name: Outbound phone call
phone.tag: For respondents who won't fill a form
phone.body: Vapi integration. telepace dials the number, warms up, runs the outline, hangs up gracefully.

hotline.name: Inbound hotline
hotline.body: Attach a Twilio number to a campaign. Anyone who calls gets your interview.

cta.title: Hear it for yourself.
cta.lede: A 60-second live interview with an AI researcher who's had a good day.

hero.lede: We're early: five people, ten thousand users, one massive market. If your best work is still ahead of you, so is ours.

howWeWork.title: Four things we won't compromise on.

craft.title: Craft > scale
craft.description: We'd rather have 200 users who love us than 20,000 who forget us.

direct.title: Direct over polite
direct.description: Kind. Direct. Specific. Nothing rots faster than unspoken feedback.

ownOutcome.title: Own the outcome
ownOutcome.description: Titles are shorthand. You own what you touch until it works.

sleep.title: Sleep matters
sleep.description: No hero-mode after 8pm. If it's on fire on Sunday, that's a design flaw.

offer.title: What we offer.
offer.compensation: Salary at the 75th percentile of Series A, with meaningful equity — not the 0.05% kind.
offer.benefits: Full health / dental / vision. $2,000 home-office budget. Two offsites a year.
offer.remote: Remote-first, async by default, four hours of live overlap.
offer.trial: We hire quickly and hire slowly: 90-day trial, then either fully in or generously out.

openRoles.title: Roles we're hiring for right now.

voiceMlEngineer.title: Founding voice ML engineer
voiceMlEngineer.summary: Own the voice pipeline end to end: STT streaming, VAD, endpointing, TTS, phone integration. Ship in weeks, iterate in hours.

designer.title: Founding designer
designer.summary: Set the visual and interaction language for the whole product. You've shipped work that felt inevitable in retrospect.

appliedResearchEngineer.title: Applied research engineer (LLM)
appliedResearchEngineer.summary: Fine-tune, eval, and deploy the Interviewer. You care about calibration, refusal behavior, and reproducibility more than benchmarks.

gtmSales.title: GTM · founding sales
gtmSales.summary: First non-technical hire. Own the pipeline from cold outbound to closed-won. Sell to PMs and researchers you'd want to be friends with.
gtmSales.stack: You've done this at a Series A before.

noRole.title: Don't see your role?
noRole.body: Send a short note to <link>{email}</link> with what you'd want to own.

hero.title: Every ship, every week.
hero.lede: Small teams building for small teams. We ship on Wednesdays and share what changed.

mcpAskFollowup.title: MCP v1.2 · ask_followup ships
mcpAskFollowup.body: Your agents can now mine any transcript corpus with a natural-language question. Non-destructive, includes supporting quotes, and returns a next_actions hint every time.

items.2: Rate limit: 60 req/min per campaign on Pro; 300/min on Team

voiceInterviewsGa.title: Voice interviews leave beta
voiceInterviewsGa.body: Browser voice + outbound phone now general availability. New endpoint metric round trip median dropped from 620ms to 380ms.

items.0: Sub-400ms turn-taking on all supported regions
items.1: 10 languages: EN · ES · FR · DE · JA · ZH · PT · IT · KO · RU

coverageTrackerRewrite.title: Coverage tracker rewrite
coverageTrackerRewrite.body: The Interviewer now knows what's still open with 3× the precision. Fewer redundant probes, faster wrap-ups, better completion rates.

notionLinearIntegrations.title: Notion + Linear integrations
notionLinearIntegrations.body: One-click push of insights straight into your existing tools. Idempotent, HMAC-signed, resumable.

publicLaunch.title: Telepace 1.0 · public launch
publicLaunch.body: After eight months of private beta, we're open. Voice-native, agent-first user research infrastructure.

cta.title: Want something we don't have yet?
cta.body: Email <email></email> — a founder reads every one.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance
acceptance.body: By creating a telepace account or using the service, you agree to these Terms. If you're using it on behalf of a company, you represent you have authority to bind that company.

service.title: 2. The service
service.body: Telepace provides software to design, run, and analyze user-research interviews. We may change features to improve the product; we won't remove features that materially affect paid tiers without 30 days' notice.

yourContent.title: 3. Your content
yourContent.body: You retain all rights to studies you design and transcripts you collect. You grant us a limited license to process this content solely to provide the service. We do not use your content to train models.

acceptableUse.title: 4. Acceptable use
acceptableUse.body: You may not use telepace to solicit interviews under false pretense, conduct research on minors without appropriate consent, or run studies in violation of applicable law. We reserve the right to suspend accounts violating these rules.

payment.title: 5. Payment
payment.body: Paid plans bill monthly or annually in advance. Refunds are prorated to the day if you cancel. Overage charges (voice minutes, completions above tier) bill in arrears within seven days of the billing cycle close.

termination.title: 6. Termination
termination.body: Either party may terminate for any reason with 30 days' notice. On termination, you get 30 days to export your data; after that, we delete it permanently.

warranty.title: 7. Warranties & disclaimers
warranty.body: The service is provided on an 'as is' basis. We warrant we'll use commercially reasonable efforts to keep the service available. We disclaim all other warranties, express or implied, to the maximum extent permitted by law.

liability.title: 8. Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, our aggregate liability arising from these Terms is limited to the amount you paid us in the 12 months preceding the claim. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

governingLaw.title: 9. Governing law
governingLaw.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of Delaware, USA. Disputes are resolved in the state and federal courts of Delaware, waiving jury trial where permitted.

changes.title: 10. Changes
changes.body: We may update these Terms; material changes trigger email notice 30 days in advance. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance.

hero.title: Plain-English privacy policy.
hero.lede: Written by humans, in complete sentences, without a single dark pattern. Effective 2026-05-05.

whatWeCollect.title: 1. What we collect
body: Account data: your name, email, and workspace metadata.
Content data: the studies you design and the interviews respondents complete.
Usage data: pages visited, features used, error traces — used only to improve the product.
We do not collect your respondents' names or contact info unless you upload them.

howWeUseIt.title: 2. How we use it
body: To run the service you signed up for — designing studies, dispatching interviews, generating insights.
To notify you about product changes and security-relevant updates.
To improve the product in aggregate — never by reading individual transcripts.
We do not sell your data. Ever.

sharing.title: 3. Who we share it with
body: Sub-processors that make the service possible: our cloud provider, our LLM vendors (only if you use our shared keys), our voice/telephony partner.
A full sub-processor list is published at telepace.io/subprocessors.
We notify you 30 days before adding a new sub-processor.

yourRights.title: 4. Your rights
body: You can export every event in your workspace at any time (GDPR Article 20).
You can delete your workspace and have all data purged within 30 days (Article 17).
You can object to specific processing (Article 21) — email privacy@telepace.io.
EU residents may lodge a complaint with their local supervisory authority.

retention.title: 5. Retention
body: Active workspace data is retained for as long as your account is active.
After deletion, backups purge within 35 days.
Anonymized aggregate telemetry may persist indefinitely.

changes.title: 6. Changes to this policy
body: Material changes trigger an email 30 days before they take effect.
Non-material clarifications are announced in the changelog.
The current version is always at this URL.

hero.title: Teams that ship <accent>informed decisions.</accent>
hero.lede: A few product teams, a few agencies, a few solo founders. What they share: they refused to guess.

northstar.quote: We ran 68 interviews in four days before a pricing change. That would have been six weeks with a research agency — and we would have shipped the wrong tier.
northstar.metric: $1.2M ARR saved from a pricing mistake

radicle.quote: Our clients get insight decks now instead of survey CSVs. Telepace pays for itself on the first project.

pentagramLabs.quote: The MCP integration means our team's Claude can actually do research now. It's a different kind of tool — an intern that never sleeps.
pentagramLabs.metric: 40 hrs / month of PM time reclaimed

cta.title: Ready to write yours?
cta.lede: If you ship a decision this month, we'd like it to be an informed one.

security.lede: Security is easy to talk about and hard to deliver. Here's the truth about what we do, what we don't, and what's next.

dataIsolation.title: Data isolation
dataIsolation.description: Every customer's data lives in its own logical tenant, keyed on org_id. Row-level policies enforce isolation at the database layer, not just the app layer.

encryption.title: Encryption
encryption.description: TLS 1.3 in transit. AES-256-GCM at rest. Envelope-encrypted secrets via cloud KMS. Zero plaintext credentials on disk.

piiRedaction.title: PII redaction
piiRedaction.description: All transcripts pass through a Presidio + custom-pattern redactor before persistence. Names, phone numbers, SSN, CC — never stored raw.

accessControl.title: Access control
accessControl.description: SSO (Okta, Google Workspace, Azure AD) on Team+. Fine-grained roles: viewer, editor, admin. Every access is audit-logged.

modelTraining.title: Model training
modelTraining.description: Your data is never used to train models — ours or any vendor's. BYO LLM keys route directly, bypassing our proxy entirely.

incidentResponse.title: Incident response
incidentResponse.description: 24/7 on-call rotation. Notification within 72 hours for any incident affecting customer data. Postmortems public by default.

certifications.title: Where we are — and where we're headed.

gdpr.name: GDPR
gdpr.status: DPA available on request · EU data residency on Team+

hipaa.name: HIPAA
hipaa.status: BAA available on Enterprise · roadmap Q1 2027

disclosure.title: Found something? Tell us.
disclosure.reportText: Report vulnerabilities to <link>security@telepace.com</link>. We acknowledge within 24 hours, triage within 72, and pay bounties starting at $500 for confirmed issues (up to $10k for critical).

demo.title: Pretend you're a user.
demo.lede: This is exactly what a respondent sees. The interviewer probes, tracks coverage, and knows when to stop. Type a few replies and get a feel.

script.0: Hi, thanks for taking a minute. What were you hoping to accomplish when you signed up for telepace?
script.1: That's a common thread. What made you stop trying to hire a full-time researcher — was it budget, or something else?
script.2: Interesting. If a magic wand solved the interview-scheduling headache, what would 'solved' look like for you?
script.3: Perfect. Last one — what have you already tried that didn't quite work?
script.4: That's incredibly helpful. Thank you. I've saved this and the team will read every word.

chat.placeholder: Type a reply and press Enter…

done.title: That's a full interview.
done.body: In a real study, telepace runs 100 of these in parallel and clusters the answers into themes.

items.0: Interviewer chooses each follow-up from your outline + prior turns
items.1: Coverage tracker knows what's still open
items.3: Escalation policy stops the run if it detects distress

voice.body: The real interviewer is fully voice-capable — browser, outbound phone, inbound hotline.
